page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the n2 family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware.

Benchmark data positions this instance in the average performance tier across most compute and memory metrics. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 30097.54 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10454.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1639.21 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 47.28 GB/sec. For database operations, the server processes 26.96 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 11561.3 on PassMark database operations. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with the 70B parameter model prompt processing speed dropping to a weak tier of 5.88 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-highmem-32 offers a high density of memory resources relative to its compute allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The balanced allocation of 8 GB of RAM per vCPU is well-suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, data compilation, and medium-sized language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz with 16 physical cores, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 10454.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 26.96 million operations per second. It delivers a cached memory read bandwidth of 1639.21 GB/sec. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and average compute capabilities, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and software compilation, though it exhibits weak performance on large-scale 70B LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and average benchmark performance suited for database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring 256 GB of RAM and 32 dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Average database performance with 26.96 million Redis SET operations per second.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation.
